name: Herbed Focaccia
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup warm water (110°F) plus 2 tbsp warm water (110°F)
2 tbsp calamata olives, pitted and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1/2 tbsp salt
2 cups bread flour
1 cup yellow onion, in thin strips
1 1/2 tbsp extra virgin olive oil
1 tsp fresh thyme, chopped
1 tsp fresh rosemary, chopped
1 pepper
1/4 cup cornmeal
1 coarse salt and pepper
Sponge:
1. In a small bowl, combine 1/4 cup warm water and pinch of sugar. Sprinkle yeast over the water mixture. Let stand until yeast dissolves and bubbles.
2. While yeast is dissolving, sauté chopped onion in 1 1/2 tbsp hot olive oil over medium heat until soft. Stir in basil, thyme, rosemary, sage, and freshly ground pepper to taste. Cook, stirring gently, for 1 minute.
3. Pour the mixture into a buttered 4-quart mixing bowl.
Dough:
1. Stir 1/2 tbsp salt into bread flour mixture.
2. Stir enough of the remaining flour with a wooden spoon until dough forms a ball.
3. Turn dough out on a well-floured surface. Knead until smooth and elastic, about 8-10 minutes, adding more of the remaining flour if needed to keep dough from sticking.
4. Place dough in an oiled bowl, turning once to oil the surface. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 30 minutes.
Topping:
1. In a small skillet, sauté onion strips in 1 1/2 tbsp hot olive oil over medium heat until soft.
2. Add thyme, rosemary, and freshly ground pepper to taste. Cook, stirring gently, for 1 minute. Remove from heat and set aside.
Assembly:
1. Sprinkle 2 tbsp of cornmeal in the baking dish, tilting to coat without shaking out excess.
2. Turn dough into the center of the baking dish. Sprinkle remaining cornmeal evenly over the dough.
3. Gently push dough out from the center towards the sides and ends of the dish, pulling carefully at the ends of the dough to fill all corners of the baking dish. Ensure the dough fills the dish evenly.
4. Using a brush, dab the top of the dough with olive oil. Lightly prick the dough at 1-inch intervals with a fork.
5. Spoon topping over the dough, spreading to within 1 inch of the edge of the dough.
6. Sprinkle with freshly ground pepper and coarse salt. Let rise, uncovered, in a warm place until doubled, about 45 minutes.
Baking:
7. Preheat oven to 425°F.
8. Bake at 425°F for 30 minutes.
9. Run a knife around the edge of the dish to loosen the bread.
10. Using two spatulas, remove from the dish and cool on a rack.
11. Cut into squares to serve.
